name: Inland Valley
description_short: Inland Power & Light is an electric utility cooperative serving 47,000 members across 13 counties in eastern Washington and northern Idaho since 1938.
description_long:
Inland Power & Light was organized in 1937 by rural citizens determined to bring electricity to farming communities that investor-owned utilities had bypassed due to sparse population. On April 27, 1938, the cooperative began serving 160 farms northeast of Spokane, marking the start of what would become the largest electric cooperative in Washington state. Today, Inland Power operates as a member-owned cooperative rather than a for-profit utility, serving approximately 47,000 meters across 13 counties in eastern Washington and northern Idaho.

Inland Power provides electrical service and related consumer financial support programs to its residential and farm members. Beyond standard utility billing, the company offers SmartHub online account management and mobile app access for viewing accounts and making payments. The cooperative provides a bill assistance program for members facing financial hardship, energy audit services to help reduce consumption, a 5% low-interest financing program for energy-saving home improvements, and resources like their monthly newsletter and annual reports to keep members informed.

What distinguishes Inland Power is its cooperative structure—owned and operated by its members rather than external investors. The company explicitly frames its mission around member welfare, offering financial assistance programs and energy efficiency support that prioritize affordability and sustainability. Their long operational history since 1938 demonstrates stability and deep community roots in rural regions historically underserved by commercial utilities.

Inland Power is a utility company, not a consumer finance lender or credit service provider. While they offer limited financial support through their bill assistance program and energy-saving financing, their primary function is electricity delivery. Members should contact them for utility services and bill assistance, not for credit products, personal loans, or credit repair services. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Electrical utility service delivery to 47,000 meters across 13 counties
Online account management and bill payment via SmartHub website
Mobile app for account access and payment management
Outage reporting and real-time outage center information
Bill assistance program for qualified members experiencing financial hardship
No-charge home energy audit service and assessment
5% low-interest financing program for energy-saving home improvements
Paperless billing option to reduce waste and costs
Monthly newsletter (Light Reading) with cooperative updates
Annual report for member transparency and engagement
Service transfer and account registration services
Customer service via phone and email support
pros: Cooperative ownership structure means profits benefit members rather than external shareholders
Bill assistance program specifically designed for members facing temporary financial hardship
No-charge home energy audit service to help members identify efficiency improvements
5% low-interest financing program for energy-saving home improvements (below market rates)
Online SmartHub platform and mobile app for 24/7 account access and payment management
Largest electric cooperative in Washington state with 47,000+ meters and stable operations since 1938
Serves underserved rural areas in 13 counties across eastern Washington and northern Idaho
cons: Bill assistance program limited to one application per calendar year per member
Energy-saving financing available only for qualified home improvement projects, not general use
Assistance allowance covers only current amount due, not past-due balances or future bills
Geographic service limited to 13 specific counties; not available outside service territory
Not a consumer finance provider; cannot offer personal loans, credit cards, or credit repair services
best_for: Rural residential customers in eastern Washington and northern Idaho needing electricity service
Homeowners seeking low-interest financing (5%) for energy efficiency upgrades
Members experiencing temporary financial difficulty qualifying for bill assistance
Farm operators and rural communities in underserved areas seeking reliable utility service
diagnosis: Inland Power & Light is best for rural residents in eastern Washington and northern Idaho who are members of the cooperative and need electricity service or utility bill assistance. Important caveat: This is a utility company, not a consumer finance provider—they do not offer credit products, personal loans, debt relief, or credit services beyond bill assistance for hardship situations.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
